ACE Fire LA — Dashboard Team GuideUpdated July 2026
1 Home2 Drawer3 Texts 4 Conversations5 EmailRulesCheat sheet

Five screens. That’s the whole job.

Tap the numbered dots on each screenshot — they light up the matching step. Tap a screenshot to zoom. Five new things shipped this weekend, marked NEW.

Auto name stampingNo more picking yourself — sign in and everything is signed for you. Open / Done inboxFinish a thread, mark it Done. It reopens itself if they reply. Reminder queueSee what the robot will send — and Skip anyone you’ve handled. Contact preferencesText-only, email-only, do-not-contact — enforced for you. Replies go to…Route the client’s answer to Scheduling, Support, or Info.

1The home screen

  1. 1
    The dark bar is today’s four urgent numbers — expired quotes, overdue invoices, reports not sent, jobs to schedule. Tap one to jump straight to those items.
  2. 2
    KPI cards summarize the pipeline. Tap to filter the list; tap again to clear.
  3. 3
    Filter chips slice the quotes: Active, Expiring ≤7d, Expired, No follow-up, Closed.
  4. 4
    Contact chips on every row — Email, Text, Call. After you reach out they stamp themselves (“Emailed · Sent 7/8”) so nobody double-contacts a client.

NEW Your name is stamped automatically on everything you send — the old “Sent by” picker is gone. Just sign in as yourself.

2The drawer — edit anything

  1. 1
    Tap any row and the drawer slides in. Call · Text · Draft email · History sit at the top — History shows every change and lets you undo a mistake.
  2. 2
    Contact preference NEW — set “No text (landline)” or “Do not contact” here and the dashboard enforces it everywhere, auto-reminders included.
  3. 3
    Tap the date chip to stamp today’s follow-up in one tap.
  4. 4
    Save, or Save + next to keep moving through a filtered list.
  1. 5
    To close a quote, scroll to Outcome and pick why: Won, Went with competitor, No longer needed, Too expensive, Postponed, No response, or Bad contact info. Closed quotes leave the active counts — review them any time under the Closed chip.

3Texts — your inbox + the robot’s queue

  1. 1
    Open / Unread / Done / All NEW — work the Open list. Mark a thread Done when it’s handled; if the client replies again it reopens by itself.
  2. 2
    Upcoming auto-reminders NEW — exactly what will send automatically, and when.
  3. 3
    Already handled that client yourself today? Skip this one stops that reminder.
  4. 4
    Skipped by mistake? Restore puts it back. Rows marked Excluded are blocked by a contact preference — leave them be.
  5. 5
    Threads with a “new” badge have unread replies — open, read, answer.

4A conversation — the whole story

  1. 1
    Everything with a client in one timeline. Emails we sent appear as cards…
  2. 2
    …and texts as chat bubbles — theirs on the left, ours on the right.
  3. 3
    ✓ Done closes the thread (Reopen brings it back). Unknown number? Tap Name to label it for everyone.
  4. 4
    Reply right here — it sends from our company number with your name stamped automatically.

5Sending an email

  1. 1
    Send from picks the right alias for the job.
  2. 2
    Replies go to NEW — Scheduling, Support, or Info. Pick the team that should receive the client’s answer.
  3. 3
    Subject and message are pre-written from the quote’s status — tweak the wording if you like.
  4. 4
    Send. It’s logged on the record and shows up in the client’s conversation thread.
Two rules

1. The Auto-SMS pill in the header (Off / Preview / Live) is managed by Carlos only — never change it. If reminders look wrong, tell Carlos.
2. If a contact button is grayed out or blocked, that’s the customer’s preference. Never work around it from a personal phone or email.

Cheat sheet

I want to…Do this
See what's urgent todayDark bar up top → tap a number
Follow up on a quoteQuotes → Email / Text / Call chip on the row
Close a quote we didn't winOpen it → Outcome → pick why → Save
Review closed quotesQuotes → Closed chip
Update a jobTracker → open it → update status → Save
Fix a mistakeOpen the record → History → undo
Reply to a client textTexts → open thread → type → Send
Clear a handled conversationTexts → ✓ Done (reopens itself if they reply)
Stop an auto-reminderTexts → Upcoming auto-reminders → Skip this one
Un-skip a reminderSame panel → Restore
Name an unknown numberOpen the thread → Name
Email a clientEmail chip → pick Replies go to → Send
Screens above show sample data. Questions, or something looks wrong? Message Carlos.
